task_completion.description: Open the target FITS image of the Eagle Nebula.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image processing software (e.g., AstroImageJ/ImageJ) is open, displaying an image of a nebula. The window title or image properties should indicate the file '656nmos.fits' is loaded.

task_completion.description: Calibrate the spatial scale to 0.1 arcsec/pixel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Set Scale' dialog or similar settings window is visible, showing the scale being set to 0.1 units per pixel (or 10 pixels = 1 unit/arcsec).

task_completion.description: Adjust the image for clarity and convert it to 8-bit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image window title or status bar explicitly shows '8-bit'. The image features (nebula pillars) should be clearly visible, indicating brightness/contrast adjustments were made.
The image is displayed in false color using the 'Fire' palette, which typically features a black/dark purple background transitioning through red and orange to bright yellow/white highlights.

task_completion.description: Include a 30-arcsecond white scale bar in the lower right corner.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct white horizontal line (scale bar) is visible in the lower right corner of the image. A 'Scale Bar' dialog might be visible configuring the width to 30 (arcsec) or 300 (pixels).

task_completion.description: Export the final image as a PNG to the specified publication directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog, file manager, or terminal window shows the file 'eagle_figure.png' being saved to or existing in the '~/AstroImages/publication/' (or '/home/ga/AstroImages/publication/') directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of an image processing application to perform the edits.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with menus, dialogs, and tools in an application like AstroImageJ or ImageJ, rather than just running a Python or ImageMagick script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The final exported image matches the manipulations performed in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual appearance of the image in the GUI (Fire LUT, 8-bit, scale bar in the lower right) should perfectly match the file being saved as the final PNG.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply download or copy a pre-made image from the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory must show the step-by-step transformation of the original grayscale FITS file into the final false-color PNG.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target FITS file: /opt/fits_samples/eagle_nebula/656nmos.fits. Output PNG path: /home/ga/AstroImages/publication/eagle_figure.png. The required spatial scale is 0.1 arcsec/pixel. The 30-arcsecond scale bar translates to exactly 300 pixels in length. The 'Fire' LUT is a specific false-color palette required by the task.
